proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_012 (continuous, range 30-90, mean ~65) is positively associated with pfs_months across the full cohort.
feature_005 (ordinal with levels 0,1,2) shows a monotonic decreasing relationship with pfs_months: higher feature_005 levels are associated with lower pfs_months.

analyses.code: from scipy.stats import linregress; linregress(df['feature_012'], df['pfs_months'])
analyses.result_summary: Univariate linear regression: slope = +0.176 months per unit feature_012, Pearson r = +0.702, p < 1e-300. Mean pfs rises from 0.56 mo (feature_012 in 30-40) to 8.20 mo (feature_012 in 80-90). feature_012 is the strongest single predictor of pfs_months.
ANOVA F=2860.75, p~0. Linear trend slope = -1.180 mo per level (p~0). Means: level 0 = 5.64, level 1 = 4.44, level 2 = 3.29.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006=1 is associated with lower pfs_months than feature_006=0.
feature_001=1 is associated with lower pfs_months than feature_001=0.
feature_013=1 is associated with HIGHER pfs_months than feature_013=0 (this is the most positive binary main effect and is a candidate treatment indicator).
feature_029=1 is associated with higher pfs_months than feature_029=0.
feature_028=1 is associated with lower pfs_months than feature_028=0.
feature_018=1 is associated with lower pfs_months than feature_018=0.
feature_023=1 is associated with higher pfs_months than feature_023=0.

analyses.result_summary: pfs(1)=5.41 vs pfs(0)=4.31 (prev=34.9%); diff = +1.098 mo, p~0. Strongest positive binary main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_004 (continuous, 0-25) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_033 (continuous, 1-100) is negatively associated with pfs_months.
feature_024 (continuous, 1.5-5.5) is positively associated with pfs_months.

Most of the other 14 continuous features (feature_036, 031, 021, 003, 025, 027, 034, 014, 037, 017, 035, 002, 007, 010) show no clinically meaningful univariate association with pfs_months (|r| < 0.02).

analyses.result_summary: All 14 features have |r| <= 0.013 with pfs_months; only feature_010 (r=-0.013, p=4.1e-3), feature_007 (r=+0.010, p=2.5e-2), and feature_027 (r=-0.013, p=3.0e-3) reach statistical significance, but effects are negligibly small. The remaining features have p > 0.05.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for all 37 features, feature_012, feature_005, feature_006, feature_013, feature_001, feature_004, feature_028, feature_024, feature_033, feature_029, and feature_018 retain large independent effects on pfs_months. Most other features lose their univariate signal and contribute little independent information.
feature_023's univariate positive effect on pfs_months is largely attributable to its strong correlation (r=+0.67) with feature_029, and shrinks toward zero after adjusting for feature_029.
feature_016's modest univariate positive effect on pfs_months is explained by its negative correlation (r=-0.36) with feature_018; after adjustment feature_016 has no independent effect.

analyses.result_summary: feature_023 univariate effect = +0.362 (p=8e-55). After adjustment for all features (esp. feature_029), feature_023 coefficient = +0.014, p=0.32. Confounded by feature_029 (Pearson r between them = +0.669).
feature_016 univariate effect = +0.114 (p=7e-7). Adjusted coefficient = +0.0019, p=0.864. Effect is fully explained by negative correlation (r=-0.36) with feature_018.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 is independently/randomly assigned with respect to all other 37 features in this dataset (i.e., it behaves like a randomized treatment indicator), in contrast to features such as feature_006, feature_001, feature_029 that act like baseline biomarkers and correlate with multiple other features.

analyses.result_summary: Multinomial logistic regression of feature_013 on the other 37 features yields McFadden pseudo-R^2 = 0.00055, no individual covariate p < 0.05. feature_013 prevalence (34.86%) is essentially independent of every other measured feature -- consistent with a randomized exposure/treatment indicator. By contrast, e.g. feature_023 vs feature_029 has r=0.67. Designating feature_013 as the candidate treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The benefit of feature_013 (treatment) on pfs_months is amplified when feature_029=1 (positive treatment-by-feature_029 interaction).
The benefit of feature_013 on pfs_months is suppressed when feature_028=1 (negative treatment-by-feature_028 interaction).
The benefit of feature_013 on pfs_months is suppressed when feature_018=1 (negative treatment-by-feature_018 interaction).
The benefit of feature_013 on pfs_months declines as feature_033 increases (negative treatment-by-feature_033 interaction).
feature_013 treatment effect is also amplified when feature_023=1, but this is partially redundant with feature_029 due to the strong correlation between feature_023 and feature_029.
Across the screen, feature_012 (the strongest baseline predictor) does NOT meaningfully modify the feature_013 treatment effect on the additive scale.

analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-feature_029 interaction coefficient = +1.559, p~0. Stratified: feature_029=0: T effect = -0.017 (p=0.63); feature_029=1: T effect = +1.577 mo (p~0).
Treatment-by-feature_028 interaction coefficient = -1.666, p~0. Stratified: feature_028=0: T effect = +1.659 mo (p~0); feature_028=1: T effect = +0.036 mo (p=0.29).
Treatment-by-feature_018 interaction coefficient = -1.338, p~0. Stratified: feature_018=0: T effect = +1.350 mo (p~0); feature_018=1: T effect = -0.014 mo (p=0.77).

Treatment-by-feature_033 interaction (feature_033 standardized): coef = -0.647 mo per SD, p~0. Quartile-stratified treatment effect: f033 Q1 (<=7.5): +1.84; Q2 (7.5-12.2): +1.92; Q3 (12.2-19.6): +0.65; Q4 (>=19.6): -0.02 mo. Effect drops sharply with rising feature_033.

Treatment-by-feature_023 interaction = +1.030, p~0. feature_023=0: T effect = +0.40 (p=2e-29); feature_023=1: T effect = +1.49 (p~0). Pearson(feature_023, feature_029)=+0.669, suggesting feature_029 is the primary driver.

Treatment-by-feature_012 interaction (standardized) = -0.016, p=0.13 (not significant after adjustment for the strong main effects). feature_012 is a strong prognostic factor but not a treatment-effect modifier.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Stacking the binary modifier conditions: the feature_013 treatment effect on pfs_months is concentrated in patients with feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0, while the complement shows little or no treatment effect.

Adding feature_018=0 to the subgroup further sharpens the contrast: in the triple subgroup (feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0) the treatment effect is much larger than the marginal effect, while in the complement the treatment effect approaches zero.

analyses.result_summary: Triple subgroup feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0: n=18681 (37.4%), T=1 mean=7.209, T=0 mean=4.300, diff=+2.908 mo, p~0. Complement: n=31319, T=1 mean=4.319, T=0 mean=4.316, diff=+0.003 mo, p=0.91. Almost all treatment effect is in the triple subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the triple subgroup (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0), the treatment effect is governed by a sharp cutpoint in feature_033 near 14: the treatment effect is large (+~5 mo) below the cutpoint and essentially zero above it.

analyses.result_summary: Within triple subgroup, treatment effect by feature_033 quartile: Q1 (<=7.5): +4.93; Q2 (7.5-12.2): +4.97; Q3 (12.2-19.6): +1.72; Q4 (>=19.6): -0.05 mo. Narrow-bin scan shows abrupt change between feature_033 in (13,14] (treated mean ~9.4, untreated ~4.2 → diff +5.2) and feature_033 in (14,15] (treated ~4.3, untreated ~4.3 → diff +0.05). Threshold ~14.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The complete subgroup defining feature_013 (treatment) responsiveness for pfs_months is feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033<14. Within this subgroup the treatment increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months. Outside this subgroup, the treatment has essentially no effect on pfs_months.
In the complement of the responder subgroup, the treatment effect on pfs_months is null (point estimate near zero, no statistical significance).

analyses.result_summary: Complement: n=39248, T=1 mean=4.316, T=0 mean=4.320, diff=-0.004 mo, p=0.485.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0 & feature_033<14), the treatment effect of feature_013 on pfs_months is largely homogeneous: no further binary feature substantially modifies it (no further binary gating is required).

Within the responder subgroup, no continuous feature meaningfully modifies the treatment effect either; the small statistical signal from feature_012 (~+0.05 mo per SD) is negligible relative to the +5 mo main effect.

feature_023's subgroup-modifying signal is captured by feature_029 once the full subgroup is conditioned on; restricting to feature_023=1 only marginally tightens the treatment effect (+5.0 vs +4.9 mo).

analyses.code: For each binary feature f, OLS(pfs ~ T + f + T*f + covariates) within the responder subgroup
analyses.result_summary: All within-subgroup binary modifier interactions have |coef| <= 0.04. Only feature_006 reaches significance (-0.043, p=4.8e-9), but the magnitude is negligible compared to the +4.94 mo main subgroup effect. No further binary modifier needed.
All within-subgroup continuous modifier interactions (per SD): |coef| <= 0.05. feature_012: +0.049 (p=1.3e-48); all others <= 0.011. No clinically meaningful modification.

029=1 & 028=0 & 018=0 & f033<14 alone: diff=+4.944. Adding feature_023=1: n=9165, diff=+4.997. Substituting feature_023=1 for feature_029=1: n=9864, diff=+4.670. feature_029 is the primary modifier; feature_023 adds little.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_006, feature_005, feature_001, feature_024, feature_012, and feature_004 are PURE prognostic factors -- they predict pfs_months identically in both treatment and control arms.

feature_029, feature_028, feature_018, and feature_033 are PURE predictive (treatment-effect-modifying) factors -- they have essentially no prognostic effect in the control arm but emerge as strong effects only in the treated arm, reflecting the interaction with treatment.

analyses.code: OLS arm-stratified: pfs ~ all features within (T=0) and within (T=1)
analyses.result_summary: Control arm R^2=0.992. Coefficients: feature_006=-1.555, feature_005=-1.166, feature_001=-0.967, feature_024=+0.488, feature_012=+0.176, feature_004=-0.0775. Treated arm shows nearly identical coefficients for these features (e.g., feature_006=-1.532, feature_005=-1.194, feature_012=+0.175). Concordance confirms pure prognostic role.

Control-arm coefficients for feature_029, feature_028, feature_018, feature_033 are negligible (|coef|<0.01, p>0.03). In the treated arm they become large: feature_029=+1.497, feature_028=-1.646, feature_018=-1.328, feature_033=-0.053 per unit. Predictive-only effect signature.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responder subgroup (feature_029=1 & feature_028=0 & feature_018=0 & feature_033<14), the control-arm pfs (T=0) is no different from the control-arm pfs in the complement, confirming that the modifiers are not prognostic.
Conversely, in the responder subgroup the treated-arm pfs (T=1) is substantially higher than treated-arm pfs in the complement, reflecting the differential treatment benefit.

analyses.result_summary: Mean pfs in responder vs complement (T=0 only): responder T=0 mean=4.284, complement T=0 mean=4.320, diff=-0.036 mo, two-sample t-test p~0.49 (not significant).

Mean pfs in responder vs complement (T=1 only): responder T=1 mean=9.228, complement T=1 mean=4.316, diff=+4.913 mo, p~0. The differential treatment benefit is concentrated entirely in the responder subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship of feature_033 with treatment effect is not strictly linear: treatment effect is plateau-flat (~+5 mo) for feature_033 < ~13 and then drops abruptly to zero between feature_033=14 and feature_033=16, suggesting a threshold/cutpoint biomarker rather than a continuous gradient.

analyses.result_summary: In responder subgroup, treatment-arm mean pfs by 1-unit feature_033 bins is ~9.1-9.4 for bins (1,14], drops to ~4.3 in bin (14,15], and remains ~4.2-4.6 for bins (15,30]. Control-arm pfs is flat across bins (~4.2-4.4). The treatment-effect curve is best modeled with a step function at feature_033=14 rather than a continuous slope.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Replacing feature_029 with feature_023 in the subgroup definition yields a similar but slightly weaker treatment-effect pocket. This confirms feature_029 is the primary gating variable and feature_023 is a partial proxy via the +0.67 correlation between them.

analyses.result_summary: Substitute subgroup (023=1 & 028=0 & 018=0 & f033<14): n=9864, diff=+4.670 mo, p~0. Original (029=1 & 028=0 & 018=0 & f033<14): diff=+4.944 mo, p~0. Combining both (029=1 AND 023=1 ...): n=9165, diff=+4.997 mo. The marginal increment from adding 023 to 029 is small (~+0.05).

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL TREATMENT-EFFECT SUBGROUP HYPOTHESIS: feature_013 (treatment) increases pfs_months by approximately +5 months (Cohen's d ~2.3) in patients meeting ALL FOUR criteria: feature_029=1 AND feature_028=0 AND feature_018=0 AND feature_033<14. The subgroup represents ~21.5% of the cohort. Outside this subgroup, feature_013 has no effect on pfs_months.

The four modifier variables (feature_029, feature_028, feature_018, feature_033) act as effective necessary-condition gates: each unfavorable value (feature_029=0, feature_028=1, feature_018=1, or feature_033>=14) independently abolishes the treatment effect even when the other modifiers are favorable.

analyses.result_summary: Final responder subgroup: n=10752 (21.5% of cohort). Treated (n=3829): mean pfs=9.228 mo. Control (n=6923): mean pfs=4.284 mo. Difference = +4.944 mo. t-test p~0. Cohen's d = 2.28 (very large effect). Feature_005 distribution within subgroup is identical to outside (0:35%, 1:50%, 2:15%) - subgroup definition is independent of disease severity.

Single-modifier-flipped subgroup tests (each starts from responder definition): flipping feature_029 to 0 (so 029=0 & 028=0 & 018=0 & f033<14): treatment effect drops to ~0. Flipping feature_028 to 1 (029=1 & 028=1 & 018=0 & f033<14): drops to ~0. Flipping feature_018 to 1 (029=1 & 028=0 & 018=1 & f033<14): drops to ~0. Flipping feature_033>=14 (029=1 & 028=0 & 018=0 & f033>=14): drops to ~0. All four conditions are individually necessary.
